Home.visitorActivityTitle: Sign in to view your activity
Home.visitorActivityDescription: Activity history, chat with providers, payments, and reviews are only available to signed-in customers.

Onboarding.visitorDescription: Explore services and professionals without signing in.
Onboarding.customerDescription: Save activity, bookings, and family follow-up.
Onboarding.bidanDescription: Use a separate path so the customer flow stays focused.

CustomerAccess.alreadyCustomer: You are already in customer mode. Continue to the page you need.

general.title: Sign in as a customer to continue
general.description: Customer access is used for activity, bookings, family preferences, and care follow-up.

activity.title: Your activity requires a customer account
activity.description: Activity history, payments, chat, and reviews only appear after you sign in as a customer.

profile.title: Customer profile is separate from visitor mode
profile.description: Account details, family preferences, and account security are only available for signed-in customers.

booking.title: Booking is only for signed-in customers
booking.description: Sign in as a customer so schedules, payments, and care follow-up can be stored properly.

benefits.savedHistory: Activity and care status stay saved
benefits.familyContext: Chat and family context stay connected

errors.loginPhoneRequired: WhatsApp number is required for the login simulation.
errors.loginPasswordRequired: Password is required for the login simulation.
errors.registerNameRequired: Full name is required for the registration simulation.
errors.registerPhoneRequired: WhatsApp number is required for the registration simulation.

CustomerAccess.registerHelper: This form is still a frontend-only simulation. After submitting, the app will switch to customer mode without backend auth.
CustomerAccess.visitorDescription: Visitors can still explore services and professionals, but activity, booking, and customer profile are gated to this page.

bidan.title: Midwife access is separated from the customer app
bidan.description: We separate the midwife flow so customer navigation stays focused on discovery, booking, and family activity.

BidanGateway.title: The midwife portal is separated from the customer app
BidanGateway.description: In this frontend simulation, the midwife flow is intentionally not merged into the customer app so operational needs and family needs stay distinct.

capabilities.dashboardDescription: This area is better suited for practice schedules, service performance, and professional activity summaries.
capabilities.queueDescription: Incoming requests, approvals, and home-visit scheduling are better handled in a dedicated midwife portal.
capabilities.separationDescription: Customers focus on finding care and follow-up. Midwives focus on operations, capacity, and SLA.

BidanGateway.nextStepDescription: If you are a customer, go back to the customer path. If you only want to browse the catalog, continue as a visitor.

Professional.scheduleRequired: Choose a visit time before continuing with an offline booking.
Professional.offlineScheduleRequired: Choose a visit day and time before continuing the offline booking.
Professional.noFilteredServices: No services match the current filters.
Professional.noFilteredServicesHint: Try another search term or reset the filters to see more services.

Explore.title: {professional}s
Explore.tryAdjusting: Try adjusting your filters or search term.
Explore.locationPointHint: Enter coordinates or use your current location. The nearest area will be derived from this coordinate point.
Explore.invalidCoordinates: Invalid coordinates. Use latitude -90..90 and longitude -180..180.
Explore.locationBrowserUnsupported: This browser does not support location detection.
Explore.locationPermissionDenied: Location permission was denied. Enable it in your browser.
Explore.locationUnavailable: Location is currently unavailable from this device.
Explore.locationFetchFailed: Failed to detect the current location.
Explore.locationResolveFailed: Failed to map coordinates to mock location details.
Explore.locationDummyHint: Location detail is currently mocked in the frontend. It can be replaced by a reverse geocoding API later without changing the UI.

Appointments.title: Activity
Appointments.trySearching: Try searching or book a new service.

ServiceDetail.noProfessionals: No professionals currently provide this service.

careHub.title: Your family profile and care preferences
careHub.description: Manage account details, family support, and follow-up rhythms so care feels more personal, organized, and safe.
careHub.focusFallback: No special focus has been saved yet.
careHub.activeAppointment: {service} with {professional} is currently part of your follow-up flow.
careHub.noActiveAppointment: There is no active session yet. You can still prepare your preferences so the next session feels faster and more tailored.

quickActions.activityDescription: Review active sessions, progress, and family follow-up.
quickActions.exploreDescription: Discover midwives and follow-up services that fit home needs.

bidanCard.title: Midwife Path Stays Separate
bidanCard.description: If you are a midwife, use this CTA to reach a dedicated area so the customer flow does not get mixed.

menu.languageDescription: Switch between Bahasa Indonesia and English anytime.
menu.accountDescription: Primary contact, care focus, and family support.
menu.securityDescription: New password and account privacy controls.

preferences.title: Care Preferences
preferences.description: Align services with family communication, visit modes, and after-care reminders.
preferences.familyUpdatesEnabled: Schedule summaries and follow-ups are shared.
preferences.familyUpdatesDisabled: Updates stay with the primary account only.
preferences.remindersEnabled: Follow-up reminders stay active for the family.
preferences.privacyEnabled: Sensitive information is limited to the main account.
preferences.privacyDisabled: Sensitive notes may appear in the family summary.

accountSheet.title: Account Details
accountSheet.description: Complete your identity, care focus, and family contact so follow-up stays aligned.
accountSheet.focusPlaceholder: Example: home care, family education, routine follow-up
accountSheet.supportDescription: Add the one contact who is most relevant when care coordination is needed.
accountSheet.familyUpdatesToggle: Share schedule updates with family support
accountSheet.privacyToggle: Keep sensitive notes for the main account only

errors.supportContactIncomplete: Complete the family support name, relationship, and phone number.

securitySheet.title: Change Password
securitySheet.description: Account security matters to keep family data and care history private.
securitySheet.helperDescription: Use a password that you can remember while still keeping access protected.

errors.newPasswordWeak: The new password does not meet the security rules.
errors.confirmMismatch: The password confirmation does not match.

about.title: {appName} is built for care that feels close, safe, and continuous.
about.description: The profile page is more than an identity screen. It becomes the family control center for care preferences, caregiver support, and secure access.
about.continuityDescription: Brings home care, online follow-up, and service activity together so mothers and families do not start from zero in every session.
about.familyDescription: Creates room for a support contact, shared updates, and realistic check-in rhythms at home.
about.trustDescription: Builds trust through stronger passwords, control over sensitive information, and a transparent experience.
about.researchLabel: this direction follows principles of continuous postnatal care, individualized care, and family support.

booking.online: Online booking created. Continue to scheduling and payment.
booking.homeVisit: Home care request submitted. The system will check coverage and confirm the schedule.
booking.onsite: Visit reservation created. Choose an available appointment slot.

alerts.chatUnavailable: Chat is only available for active activities.

appointment.welcomeTemplate: Hello, how can I help you with your {serviceName} reservation?

statusBanners.requested: The provider is reviewing your request. We will notify you as soon as it is approved.
statusBanners.paid: Payment received. The provider will follow the selected schedule.
statusBanners.confirmed: Your schedule is confirmed. Please prepare the location and anything relevant before the session starts.
statusBanners.inService: The session is in progress. You can still send a message if anything else is needed.
statusBanners.cancelled: This reservation has been cancelled. You can book a new schedule anytime.
statusBanners.rejected: This request was declined. Try another slot or a different available provider.
statusBanners.expired: This request expired because the payment was not completed in time.

home.emptyStateDescription: Use the service catalog to start a new flow from discovery to booking.

review.title: Leave a Review
review.helperText: Your review helps us improve service quality.
review.reviewPlaceholder: The service was very friendly and professional...
review.successAlert: Review submitted successfully. Thank you for your feedback.

Integration.title: Backend Contract + Realtime Example
Integration.description: This page demonstrates REST access through the shared SDK generated from the backend OpenAPI document, plus realtime chat over WebSocket.
Integration.restError: Failed to load backend data. Make sure the backend is running on localhost:8080.
Integration.emptyMessages: No live messages yet. Send one message to verify the websocket flow.
Integration.messagePlaceholder: Send a live message to the backend demo thread...
Integration.socketError: WebSocket connection failed. Verify the backend websocket endpoint and allowed origin.
Integration.noteContract: The OpenAPI document is exported from backend route registration, not hand-maintained in frontend.
Integration.noteSdk: Frontend integration uses the shared SDK package so fetch usage stays typed at development time.
Integration.noteWebsocket: Realtime chat is intentionally separated from OpenAPI because websocket transport needs its own event contract.
